summary: Matched the sysdolphin baselib DObj unit by replacing asm/sysdolphin/baselib/dobj.s with src/sysdolphin/baselib/dobj.c, adding DObj and MObj headers, wiring the build to compile the C object, and propagating newly identified HSD_DObj, HSD_MObj, HSD_PObj, and class-system symbol names through assembly callsites and global variable labels. Several difficult DObj routines remained as inline asm under NON_MATCHING guards, with comments citing string section or extern issues, while simpler wrappers and traversal logic were expressed in C.
what_changed: Deleted the old 614-line asm/sysdolphin/baselib/dobj.s and added a 520-line C implementation at src/sysdolphin/baselib/dobj.c.
Added src/sysdolphin/baselib/dobj.h defining HSD_DObj, HSD_DObjDesc, HSD_DObjInfo, HSD_ShapeAnimDObj, HSD_DOBJ/HSD_DOBJ_INFO/HSD_DOBJ_METHOD macros, and public DObj API prototypes.
Added src/sysdolphin/baselib/mobj.h with MObj animation constants, material animation opcodes, structure declarations, and MObj API surface needed by DObj and related code.
Changed obj_files.mk from building asm/sysdolphin/baselib/dobj.o to src/sysdolphin/baselib/dobj.o, and added src/sysdolphin/baselib/dobj.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED in e_files.mk.
Renamed DObj globals in asm/data.s: lbl_80405450 became hsdDObj and now points to DObjInfoInit; lbl_80407590 became hsdClass.

Updated many assembly callsites to use identified DObj names, including HSD_DObjSetFlags, HSD_DObjClearFlags, HSD_DObjModifyFlags, HSD_DObjAddAnimAll, HSD_DObjReqAnimAll, HSD_DObjLoadDesc, HSD_DObjRemoveAll, and HSD_DObjResolveRefsAll.

Updated MObj and PObj-related unknown function labels in include/functions.h and callsites, including HSD_MObjAnim, HSD_MObjRemoveAnimByFlags, HSD_MObjLoadDesc, HSD_PObjLoadDesc, HSD_PObjRemoveAnimAllByFlags, HSD_PObjAddAnimAll, HSD_PObjReqAnimAllByFlags, HSD_PObjAnimAll, HSD_PObjRemoveAll, and HSD_PObjResolveRefsAll.
Named class-system helpers hsdInitClassInfo, hsdNew, and hsdSearchClassInfo, and updated class.s/cobj.s/fog.s/jobj.s/lobj.s/mobj.s/pobj.s/tobj.s/wobj.s references accordingly.
Adjusted class.h so HSD_CLASS_METHOD casts an arbitrary object to HSD_Class before reading class_info, and added a next_p(p) helper macro for linked-list traversal.

Implemented DObj operations in C for current DObj tracking, flags get/set/clear/modify, animation removal/add/request/step over DObj lists, descriptor loading, removal, allocation, reference resolution, display, release, amnesia, and class-info initialization, though some functions are retained as asm inside #else branches.
smart_moves: The PR did not only match dobj.c in isolation; it propagated the symbol discoveries through existing assembly users, making future cross-system decompilation easier to search and reason about.
Using HSD_DObjInfo hsdDObj and HSD_Class hsdClass labels in data.s tied class metadata to real C-side names, which is useful for later baselib class hierarchy work.
The added dobj.h captures exact field offsets for HSD_DObj: parent at 0x00, next at 0x04, mobj at 0x08, pobj at 0x0C, aobj at 0x10, and flags at 0x14.
The DObj list APIs consistently use null-safe early returns and for-loop traversal over next pointers, matching patterns reusable for other HSD object lists.
The PR used NON_MATCHING C versions as documentation while preserving matching with inline asm for functions blocked by section/string/extern issues.

DObj behavior was decomposed around lower-level MObj/PObj/AObj APIs rather than duplicating work, making names like HSD_DObjAddAnimAll and HSD_DObjReqAnimAllByFlags self-validating through their callees.
The next_p(p) macro in class.h supports parallel traversal of linked animation lists with possible NULL inputs, as seen in HSD_DObjAddAnimAll.
decomp_lessons: When a matched unit exposes many previously unknown symbols, update assembly callsites and include prototypes in the same PR so the rest of the tree benefits immediately.
For HSD class-based objects, name both the global class info object and the init function; these data labels often unlock hsdNew/hsdInitClassInfo usage and method-table offsets.
Headers for adjacent systems may be needed before the target unit can be cleanly expressed; here DObj matching required MObj and PObj function/struct knowledge.
Keep C versions of nonmatching functions under NON_MATCHING when they explain intent, but use inline asm fallbacks to preserve the build and matching status.

String placement and extern layout can be matching blockers; comments like 'Non-matching due to section of the strings' and 'Non-matching because a mess of externs' are useful breadcrumbs for future cleanup.
Class method access macros must be structurally correct for inherited HSD objects; HSD_CLASS_METHOD was changed to cast to HSD_Class before reading class_info.
Descriptor-loading functions in baselib commonly recurse through next descriptors, allocate via hsdNew or a default class object, then dispatch through a method-table load function.
naming_conventions: HSD_DObj* functions use Pascal-style subsystem prefixes, e.g. HSD_DObjSetFlags, HSD_DObjClearFlags, HSD_DObjModifyFlags, HSD_DObjLoadDesc, HSD_DObjResolveRefsAll.
Class info globals use lower camel-style hsd names, e.g. hsdDObj and hsdClass.
Class helper functions use lower camel-style hsd names, e.g. hsdNew, hsdSearchClassInfo, hsdInitClassInfo.
Struct typedefs follow HSD_* names over underlying _HSD_* tags, e.g. struct _HSD_DObj typedefed to HSD_DObj.
Object-cast and class-info macros use HSD_DOBJ, HSD_DOBJ_INFO, and HSD_DOBJ_METHOD.
Linked-list bulk functions carry an All suffix, e.g. HSD_DObjRemoveAnimAllByFlags, HSD_DObjAddAnimAll, HSD_DObjAnimAll, HSD_DObjRemoveAll.
ByFlags suffix is used for APIs that mask/select animation channels or object behavior, e.g. HSD_DObjReqAnimAllByFlags and HSD_MObjRemoveAnimByFlags.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced the asm object in obj_files.mk with the C object only after adding the needed C source and headers.
Used inline asm implementations inside #else branches for DObjLoad, HSD_DObjLoadDesc, HSD_DObjAlloc, and DObjInfoInit while retaining NON_MATCHING C bodies as intent documentation.
Preserved matching when symbol names changed by editing assembly labels and branch targets without changing machine code bytes.
Renamed global data labels hsdDObj and hsdClass while keeping their addresses stable, enabling cleaner lis/addi relocation labels in assembly.
Used #pragma push/#pragma peephole on around small inline helper sections such as hsdDelete/HSD_DObjRemove and release/amnesia helpers, suggesting compiler-output sensitivity.
For flag modification, both public and inline versions use the expression pattern dobj->flags = dobj->flags & ~mask | flags & mask, matching observed codegen needs.
For linked animation traversal, HSD_DObjAddAnimAll advances DObj, MatAnim, and ShapeAnimDObj lists together using dp = dp->next and next_p for possibly-null animation lists.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove the inline asm/NON_MATCHING fallbacks in DObjLoad, HSD_DObjLoadDesc, HSD_DObjAlloc, or DObjInfoInit.
Search for later cleanup of string-section issues around dobj.c and lbl_80405494.
Search for PRs that fully decompile HSD_MObj and HSD_PObj after the function names introduced here.
Check whether hsdDelete was later moved into class.h or a shared class implementation instead of remaining inline/local.
Look for later revisions to HSD_DObjInfo, especially the update callback field and method-table offsets.
